faqs.question: Are yellow jackets more aggressive than other wasps?
faqs.answer: Yes, yellow jackets are among the most aggressive stinging insects. Unlike honeybees, they can sting multiple times and will vigorously defend their nests. They're particularly dangerous in late summer when colonies are largest, making professional removal essential for safety.

faqs.question: Do you handle yellow jacket nests in hard-to-reach locations?
faqs.answer: Absolutely. Our Woodbridge team is equipped to safely remove yellow jacket nests from challenging locations including wall voids, attics, underground burrows, high eaves, and within landscaping. We have specialized equipment and protective gear to access nests wherever they're located on your property.
